content:
# 3D LUT Temporal Interpolation Design (2026-04-03)

## Decision
Replace grade_frame on non-keyframe frames with a precomputed per-depth-band 3D color LUT. 33x33x33 grid × 8 depth bands, built once per clip (~3.3MB). Captures full per-pixel pipeline (LUT apply + HSL + fused ambiance + warmth + blend). Clarity skipped on non-keyframes (spatial, being replaced by Maxine).

## Key Points
- LUT is constant per clip — calibration and grade params don't change frame-to-frame
- Non-keyframe cost: depth lerp (~12ms) + LUT trilinear lookup (~15ms rayon) = ~27ms vs ~40ms current
- Future CUDA texture acceleration: ~5ms per non-keyframe
- New module: dorea-gpu/src/lut3d.rs (GradeLut, Lut3D, build/apply functions)
- New function: grade_pixel() — single-pixel pipeline without clarity
- Keyframe path unchanged (full pipeline including clarity)

## Supersedes
Temporal grade interpolation (perf v3 opt 5) — reverted in PR #23 due to ghosting from pixel-wise RGB blending.

Spec: docs/decisions/2026-04-03-3d-lut-temporal-interpolation.md
